delta.text: Build

is green. Final report:

**File and line:** `crates/lua-v

m/src/vm.rs:1112-1115` — `int_floor_mod`.

**What `
int_floor_mod` does:** Wraps Lua's integer `%` operator with floor-mod semantics (result takes sign of divisor;
raises on zero). Delegates to the existing `imod` helper in the same file; the `&mut LuaState` par
ameter exists for the call-site signature in `int_arith`/

`raw_arith`.

**Family members also implemented (same impl region, vm.rs):**
-
`int_floor_div` (line 1118) — Lua `//` on integers; delegates to existing `idiv`.

Truncates toward −∞; raises on zero.
- `float_floor_mod` (line 1123) — Lua `%` on floats; delegates
to existing `fmodf`. Returns `Result<f64, LuaError>` to match dispatcher signature.
All three were stubs in the same `// ─── Integer arithmetic ───` region and
dispatched-to from `object.rs::int_arith` / `float_arith`. The real arithmetic functions (`imod`, `id
iv`, `fmodf`) already existed directly above the stubs in vm.rs — the stubs were just the state-aware wrappers wa

iting to be hooked up.

**Newly-surfaced TODOs noticed but NOT implemented

(different family — left alone):**
- `vm.rs:179-182` — `test_a_mode`, `is_mm
_mode`, `is_vararg_prep`, `is_in_top` (Instruction/OpM

ode predicates).
- `vm.rs:2498` — `todo!("unrecognised opcode")` in VM dispatch

tail.
- `object.rs:270` — `TagMethod::from_arith_op` conversion helper (different crate, different family).

**Carg
o build -p lua-cli:** ✅ green (`Finished dev profile … in 1.89s`).
Only pre-existing warnings.

content.text:
Build is green. Final report:

**File and line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:1112-1115` — `int_floor_mod`.

**What `int_floor_mod` does:** Wraps Lua's integer `%` operator with floor-mod semantics (result takes sign of divisor; raises on zero). Delegates to the existing `imod` helper in the same file; the `&mut LuaState` parameter exists for the call-site signature in `int_arith`/`raw_arith`.

**Family members also implemented (same impl region, vm.rs):**
- `int_floor_div` (line 1118) — Lua `//` on integers; delegates to existing `idiv`. Truncates toward −∞; raises on zero.
- `float_floor_mod` (line 1123) — Lua `%` on floats; delegates to existing `fmodf`. Returns `Result<f64, LuaError>` to match dispatcher signature.

All three were stubs in the same `// ─── Integer arithmetic ───` region and dispatched-to from `object.rs::int_arith` / `float_arith`. The real arithmetic functions (`imod`, `idiv`, `fmodf`) already existed directly above the stubs in vm.rs — the stubs were just the state-aware wrappers waiting to be hooked up.

**Newly-surfaced TODOs noticed but NOT implemented (different family — left alone):**
- `vm.rs:179-182` — `test_a_mode`, `is_mm_mode`, `is_vararg_prep`, `is_in_top` (Instruction/OpMode predicates).
- `vm.rs:2498` — `todo!("unrecognised opcode")` in VM dispatch tail.
- `object.rs:270` — `TagMethod::from_arith_op` conversion helper (different crate, different family).

**Cargo build -p lua-cli:** ✅ green (`Finished dev profile … in 1.89s`). Only pre-existing warnings.

result:
Build is green. Final report:

**File and line:** `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:1112-1115` — `int_floor_mod`.

**What `int_floor_mod` does:** Wraps Lua's integer `%` operator with floor-mod semantics (result takes sign of divisor; raises on zero). Delegates to the existing `imod` helper in the same file; the `&mut LuaState` parameter exists for the call-site signature in `int_arith`/`raw_arith`.

**Family members also implemented (same impl region, vm.rs):**
- `int_floor_div` (line 1118) — Lua `//` on integers; delegates to existing `idiv`. Truncates toward −∞; raises on zero.
- `float_floor_mod` (line 1123) — Lua `%` on floats; delegates to existing `fmodf`. Returns `Result<f64, LuaError>` to match dispatcher signature.

All three were stubs in the same `// ─── Integer arithmetic ───` region and dispatched-to from `object.rs::int_arith` / `float_arith`. The real arithmetic functions (`imod`, `idiv`, `fmodf`) already existed directly above the stubs in vm.rs — the stubs were just the state-aware wrappers waiting to be hooked up.

**Newly-surfaced TODOs noticed but NOT implemented (different family — left alone):**
- `vm.rs:179-182` — `test_a_mode`, `is_mm_mode`, `is_vararg_prep`, `is_in_top` (Instruction/OpMode predicates).
